name: HMAC-SHA256 from scratch
task: Create /tmp/eval_hmsc/hmsc.py with hmac_sha256_scratch(key, message) taking key and message as bytes and returning the HMAC-SHA256 hex digest (str) WITHOUT using the hmac module (only hashlib.sha256 is allowed). Implement RFC 2104 with the SHA-256 block size B = 64 bytes: (1) if len(key) > 64, replace key with the 32-byte digest sha256(key).digest(); (2) right-pad key with zero bytes to exactly 64 bytes -> k; (3) form ipad_key = k XOR (0x36 repeated 64 times) and opad_key = k XOR (0x5c repeated 64 times), XORing byte-by-byte; (4) return sha256(opad_key + sha256(ipad_key + message).digest()).hexdigest(). Note the inner hash is fed its raw 32-byte .digest(), not its hex string.

validation.script: import importlib.util,hashlib,hmac,re; src = open('/tmp/eval_hmsc/hmsc.py').read(); code = re.sub(r'#.*', '', re.sub(r'([\'"]).*?\1', '', src)); assert not re.search(r'^\s*(?:import|from)\b[^\n]*\bhmac\b', code, re.M) and not re.search(r'\bhmac\s*\.', code), 'the hmac module is banned; implement it from scratch'; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_hmsc/hmsc.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); msg = b'The quick brown fox'; key = b'secret_key'; e = hmac.new(key, msg, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest(); a = mod.hmac_sha256_scratch(key, msg); assert e == a, f'{e} vs {a}'; long_key = b'x'*100; e2 = hmac.new(long_key, msg, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest(); a2 = mod.hmac_sha256_scratch(long_key, msg); assert e2 == a2; print('OK')
